steps.title: Grasp the tail between the base...
steps.instruction: Grasp the tail between the base and the middle section and lift the mouse clear of all surrounding objects.

steps.title: Mice are observed for the fore/hindlimb...
steps.instruction: Mice are observed for the fore/hindlimb position for 00:00:10 with 5-10 s resting time in between. If the animal curls up and holds its tail/fingers continue the recording until you have 10s of the animal in the correct position.

steps.title: If the limbs are consistently splayed...
steps.instruction: If the limbs are consistently splayed outward, away from the abdomen, it is assigned a score of 0 (see figure).

steps.title: If one limb is retracted toward...
steps.instruction: If one limb is retracted toward the abdomen for more than 50% of the time suspended, it receives a score of 1.

steps.title: If both limbs are partially retracted...
steps.instruction: If both limbs are partially retracted toward the abdomen for more than 50% of the time suspended, it receives a score of 2.

steps.title: If its limbs are entirely retracted...
steps.instruction: If its limbs are entirely retracted and touching the abdomen for more than 50% of the time suspended and with curled toes, it receives a score of 3.

steps.title: If both the front and hind...
steps.instruction: If both the front and hind limbs are retracted for more than 50% of the time suspended, it receives a score of 4.

steps.title: Place the mouse back into its...
steps.instruction: Place the mouse back into its cage and record its limb clasping score. This test is performed two days per week with two trials per session.

steps.title: Mice are habituated for 00:30:00 in...
steps.instruction: Mice are habituated for 00:30:00 in the testing room in their home cage.
